senses.definition: (of a meeting) having enough members present to conduct business or to make official decisions by voting
the minimum number of people that must be present for a meeting to officially begin or for decisions to be made

examples.x: The board meeting could not proceed as scheduled because there was not enough members present to meet the quorum requirement.
According to the organization's bylaws, at least 50% of members must be present to establish a quorum for voting on important decisions.
The committee waited for fifteen minutes past the scheduled start time to allow more members to arrive and reach the quorum.
Without a quorum, the annual general meeting had to be rescheduled for a later date to ensure all members could participate in decision-making.
It's important to achieve a quorum during meetings to ensure that decisions are made with the input of a representative group of stakeholders.

senses.definition: to unfairly compel someone or a group of people to to accept something such as a decision, rule, etc. quickly
to think again about an opinion or decision, particularly to see if it needs changing or not

examples.x: Feeling unsure, she asked her friends to help her reconsider her career path.
She reconsidered her stance on the issue after hearing new evidence.
He asked his boss to reconsider his request for a promotion.
The board is reconsidering the proposal at the next meeting.
The judge agreed to reconsider the verdict in light of the new testimony.

senses.definition: the act of thinking about an opinion or decision again, especially with an intention to change it
(usually passive) to send a matter to someone of an authority so that it can be dealt with
a firm decision to do something or to behave in a certain way, often made after careful consideration

examples.x: She made a resolution to finish writing her novel by the end of the year.
Setting a resolution to save more money each month helped him achieve his financial goals.
She made a New Year's resolution to exercise more regularly.
His resolution to quit smoking was motivated by concerns for his health.
He stuck to his resolution of reading one book per month.

senses.definition: to make a decision with determination

examples.x: After much consideration, she resolved to pursue her dream of starting her own business.
Despite the challenges ahead, he resolved to overcome them and achieve his goals.
They resolved to work together to find a solution to the problem.
In the face of adversity, she resolved to stay strong and persevere.
After the argument, they resolved to communicate more effectively to avoid misunderstandings in the future.

senses.definition: to allow people's negative comments and criticisms change one's mind about something

examples.x: He quickly retreated from his position after realizing it wasn’t well-received by the team.
The company had to retreat on their decision to raise prices, due to customer complaints.
The politician retreated from his stance on healthcare after public criticism.
His attempt to impose new regulations was met with resistance, causing him to retreat.
Faced with strong opposition, the leader decided to retreat from the controversial decision.

senses.definition: an act of changing one's mind about something because of people's negative comments and criticisms
to declare someone guilty or innocent in a court of law
to alter a previous decision, judgment, etc. to the contrary one
the act of changing an earlier promise, decision, or statement so that it becomes entirely different
to officially approve something such as a decision, resolution, etc. without proper consideration
a person or organization that automatically approves or authorizes a plan, decision, etc. without considering it properly
to make an official decision about something

examples.x: The judge is expected to rule on the case next week after reviewing all the evidence.
The board of directors will convene tomorrow to rule on the proposed merger.
The committee ruled in favor of the new zoning regulations after a series of public hearings.
The city council ruled against the construction of the new shopping mall due to environmental concerns.

senses.definition: a decision made by someone with official power, particularly a judge

examples.x: The Supreme Court's ruling on the case set a precedent for future privacy rights.
The judge's ruling favored the plaintiff, awarding them damages for the accident.
The ruling by the arbitration panel ended the long-standing dispute between the two companies.
Citizens protested against the government's ruling to increase taxes without public consultation.
The school board's ruling to implement a new dress code policy sparked controversy among parents and students.

senses.definition: the right or chance to give an opinion about something

examples.x: The committee gave everyone a say in the decision-making process to ensure all viewpoints were considered.
Employees appreciated having a say in the new company policies that would affect their daily routines.
As a valued member of the team, she always had a significant say in project planning.
The community meeting was held to give residents a say in the development plans for the new park.
In a democratic society, citizens have a say in how they are governed through voting and public discourse.

senses.definition: to decide something, after considering all possible alternatives

examples.x: After much deliberation, she settled on a color for the living room walls.
The team needs to settle on a strategy before the competition begins.
After much debate, the committee finally managed to settle on a compromise.
The couple took weeks to settle on a name for their newborn baby.

senses.definition: to postpone making a decision until the next day or a later time, often to think about it more

examples.x: Rather than rushing into an agreement, they decided to sleep on the partnership offer and reconvene later.
The team leader recommended sleeping on the new project proposal before giving a definitive answer.
Faced with a difficult choice, she opted to sleep on the decision to avoid making a hasty choice.
Before finalizing the contract, the parties agreed to sleep on the terms and discuss any revisions in the morning.
The couple agreed to sleep on whether to go on a spontaneous trip or stick to their original plans.

senses.definition: unable to choose because there are a lot of choices
easy to understand, lacking complication or ambiguity

examples.x: It's a straight choice between two options.
The instructions were straight and easy to follow.
This is a straight deal, no catches.
The rules of the house were straight and logical.
The presentation was straight, with no technical jargon.

senses.definition: to undertake an action, often involving risk or uncertainty

examples.x: She decided to take a chance on the new job offer in a different city.
Taking a chance on entrepreneurship, he started his own business.
Sometimes, taking a chance on love leads to meaningful and fulfilling relationships.
The athlete took a chance by attempting a challenging move during the competition.

senses.definition: to consider something when trying to make a judgment or decision

examples.x: When planning the budget, it is crucial to take unexpected expenses into account to ensure financial stability.

senses.definition: to give thought to a certain fact before making a decision

examples.x: Before making a decision, it's important to take all factors into consideration.

senses.definition: to give something adequate amount of thought and consideration before making a decision or forming an opinion about it
the ability to recognize something with good quality or high standard, especially in art, style, beauty, etc., based on personal preferences

examples.x: Her taste in clothing reflects her keen eye for elegant and timeless fashion.
He has a discerning taste in art, preferring minimalist pieces with subtle complexities.
The boutique caters to customers with refined tastes in luxury accessories and apparel.
Their impeccable taste in home décor is evident in the harmonious blend of colors and textures.
Developing a sophisticated taste in fashion often involves exploring different styles and understanding personal preferences.

senses.definition: used to express that one's decision is definite and cannot be changed
a choice or option that seems less harmful or unpleasant out of two that one is confronted with

examples.x: In the election, voters felt they had to choose the lesser of two evils, as both candidates had significant flaws.
The company had to decide between laying off employees or reducing salaries, opting for the lesser of two evils to minimize the impact on the workforce.
In the film, the protagonist had to decide whether to betray a friend or face dire consequences—a classic example of choosing the lesser of two evils.

senses.definition: to consider a matter carefully before reaching a decision

examples.x: She needs time to think over whether to accept the invitation.
Before buying a house, it's wise to think over your financial situation.
We should think over the consequences of our actions.

senses.definition: to think about something very carefully before doing it

examples.x: These foods are potentially dangerous, yet nobody thinks twice before handing them out to their loved ones.
Why are women thinking twice about motherhood?
Before quitting your job, you should think twice about the financial implications.
I had to think twice about buying that expensive car; it would have drained my savings.
When offering criticism, it's essential to think twice to ensure your words are constructive.

senses.definition: an unclear situation that either of two possibilities have an equal chance of happening

examples.x: It’s a toss-up whether we should go to the beach or stay home today.
With both candidates so evenly matched, it’s really a toss-up who will get the job.
It’s a toss-up whether I’ll make it to the meeting on time with this traffic.
The game was so close, it was a toss-up who would win.

senses.definition: a severely difficult situation, problem, or question
(of a group) fully in agreement on something

examples.x: The unanimous decision to relocate the office was reached after extensive discussion and consideration of all viewpoints.
The board of directors was unanimous in their support for the new company policy.
The jury delivered an unanimous verdict of guilty after carefully reviewing all the evidence.
The team was unanimous in their choice of captain for the upcoming season.
The committee reached an unanimous decision to approve the proposed budget.

senses.definition: unable to make a decision or form a definite opinion about a matter

examples.x: She remained undecided about which college to attend, weighing the pros and cons of each option.
He was still undecided about which career path to pursue after graduation.
The group was undecided about where to go for vacation, unable to agree on a destination.
She remained undecided about whether to accept the job offer or continue searching for other opportunities.
He was undecided about whether to sell his house or renovate it.
She is still undecided about which college to attend, weighing the pros and cons of each option.
Despite all the arguments presented, I am still undecided about which course of action to take.

senses.definition: (particularly of a law court) to state that a previous decision is correct

examples.x: The Supreme Court decided to uphold the lower court's ruling, affirming the original verdict.
The appeals court upheld the conviction, stating that the trial had been conducted fairly.
The board voted to uphold the committee's decision to implement the new policy.
Despite the challenge, the judge upheld the previous decision, citing strong legal precedent.
The disciplinary panel upheld the suspension after reviewing all the evidence and testimonies.

senses.definition: an official decision made by the jury in a court after the legal proceedings

examples.x: The jury reached an unanimous verdict of guilty after deliberating for several hours.
The judge read the verdict aloud in the packed courtroom.
The defense team was surprised by the unexpected verdict of not guilty.
The verdict brought closure to the long and contentious legal battle.
The media reported on the landmark verdict that set a new precedent in criminal law.

senses.definition: used to compare or to show contrast between two choices, decisions, etc.

examples.x: Cats versus dogs: which pet is more popular in the world?
The debate on nature versus nurture has been going on for centuries

senses.definition: refusal of or disagreement with something

examples.x: The president issued a veto against the proposed tax reform bill.
Her veto of the budget plan led to further negotiations among the team.
The board's veto of the project halted its development immediately.
The governor's veto of the legislation sparked a heated debate in the state assembly.
The mayor used his veto to reject the council's zoning changes.

senses.definition: the faculty to use free will and make decisions

examples.x: She chose to pursue a career in art of her own volition, following her passion rather than external pressures.
The decision to travel was made entirely of her own volition; no one pressured her into it.
He acted according to his own volition, refusing to be influenced by others' opinions.
The ability to vote is an exercise of one's volition in a democratic society.
Despite the challenges, she faced them with determination and volition, refusing to give up on her goals.

senses.definition: an official choice made by an individual or a group of people in a meeting or election

examples.x: In the upcoming election, citizens will cast their votes for mayor.
The board members took a vote on the proposed merger.
The referendum resulted in a close vote on the new school funding measure.
The union members held a secret ballot vote to elect their new president.
The committee conducted a vote to decide the winner of the design competition.

senses.definition: to decide someone’s position, status, or recognition through a voting process

examples.x: The committee voted him into the position of chairman.
She was voted out of office after the scandal broke.
After years of dedication, they voted her into the Hall of Fame.
The council voted him into the role of treasurer unanimously.
He was voted off the board after failing to meet his responsibilities.

senses.definition: to hold back and hesitate due to uncertainty

examples.x: Sarah could see him waver in his commitment to the project as the challenges grew.
Faced with the difficult decision, he began to waver on whether to accept the job offer.
As the deadline approached, Jane began to waver on whether to submit her bold proposal or play it safe.
In the face of criticism, the author didn't waver from expressing their unique perspective in the novel.

senses.definition: one who is incapable of making any decisions due to fear or uncertainty, particularly in an election
to consider all the possible outcomes and different aspects of something before making a definite decision

examples.x: Before accepting the job offer, he took the time to weigh the pros and cons, considering the impact on both his career and personal life.
When choosing a college, it's crucial to weigh factors such as location, programs offered, and tuition.
In business decisions, entrepreneurs need to weigh the potential risks and benefits to make informed choices.
Considering multiple job offers, she weighed factors like salary, company culture, and career growth potential.
As a responsible consumer, he weighs the environmental impact of products before making purchasing decisions.

word_list.pronunciation: wɛn ɪf pˈʊʃ kˈʌmz tə ʃˈʌv

senses.definition: used when a situation reaches a critical point and one must take action in order to deal with it

examples.x: We were supposed to look after each other, but when push came to shove, it was every man for himself.
They were known for always having each other's backs but when push came to shove, they acted as if they didn't even know each other.
The negotiations were tough, but when push came to shove, they reached a compromise.
They had their differences, but when push came to shove, they worked together as a team.

senses.definition: used to talk about a doubt or choice when facing two options

examples.x: He's trying to figure out whether he should study abroad or stay in his home country.
I can't decide whether to wear a dress or jeans to the event.
I'm not sure whether I should go to the party or stay home.
Jane asked her friend whether he wanted to join her for lunch or eat separately.
She asked whether he liked ice cream or cake better.
